title: REQUEST: Import .moba files, from MobaXTerm
author: Apollon-Theos
body_snippet:
v 1.76 and 1.77.2 cannot import *.moba files, saved from MobaXTerm. 
But MobaXTerm can import config file from mRemoteNG :)

## Expected Behavior
Can import *.moba files, for one connection. And *.mtxsessions for folder with multiple connections.

## Current Behavior
This files not exist in filter list for import files. With verify that is RDP or HTTP sessions.

## Possible Solution


## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)
No import :(

## Your Environment
* Version used: 1.76 every wo...

iterations.description: Importing session files from MobaXTerm (a popular remote management tool) would significantly enhance user migration and interoperability, aligning with existing import functionality for other tools like PuTTY, RoyalTS, and SecureCRT. The existing mRemoteNG/Config/Import directory and FrmQuickImport provide a clear integration path.
Bulk verified: commit ebccb513 exists (MEDIUM confidence)

comments.author: JustBeta
comments.snippet:
If you are interested, I have developed a PowerShell script to perform this conversion.
It may not be perfect, but it allowed me to convert 245 MobaXterm sessions to mRemoteNG.

Here is the link to download it: 
https://github.com/JustBeta/Export-MobaXtern2mRemoteNG/tree/main

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `ebccb513` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** REQUEST: Import .moba files, from MobaXTerm

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Importing session files from MobaXTerm (a popular remote management tool) would significantly enhance user migration and interoperability, aligning with existing import functionality for other tools like PuTTY, RoyalTS, and SecureCRT. The existing mRemoteNG/Config/Import directory and FrmQuickImport provide a clear integration path.
Approach: Create a new MobaXTermImporter.cs implementing IConnectionImporter in mRemoteNG/Config/Import, parse .moba files into mRemoteNG ConnectionInfo objects, add unit tests, and integrate the new importer into FrmQuickImport.
